t_ch: 哼，对付你这种家伙，我能用的手段多了去了，但…我凭什么告诉你？
t_en: Hmph, I've got plenty of moves against someone like you, but... why should I tell you?

t_ch: 尽管担心受怕去吧，我早晚会让你为自己的错误付出应有的代价。
t_en: You will lie awake at night, wondering what I'll do to you. I will make you pay for your mistakes sooner or later.

t_ch: 和你说话真费劲。
t_en: Talking to you is such a chore.

t_ch: 你不会真的——自我感觉良好到以为我想和你说话吧？
t_en: Are you really... that thick-headed to think that I WANTED to talk to you?

t_ch: 就这样吧，拜拜。
t_en: That's all. Bye-bye.

t_ch: 呦呵，发现自己说不过我，这就打算夹着尾巴逃跑啦？
t_en: Oh-ho, running away with your tail tucked between your legs when you're losing a verbal joust?

t_ch: 唉…这样下去学生还怎么成才？真是校风日下！
t_en: *sigh* If this goes on, how will the students be able to hone their talents? This school is becoming worse with each passing day!

t_ch: 这位老者愤怒地自言自语着，头顶的天环发出不和谐的闪动。在看见你的那刻，他立即收起动作，换上了足以示人的礼貌面容。
t_en: An elderly person is angrily muttering to himself, the halo above his head flickering discordantly with rage. However, when he spots you, he immediately stops what he's doing and puts on a polite countenance to greet you.

t_ch: 咳！失礼了。我是阿图罗，折纸大学的「代理校长」——幸会，星穹列车的无名客。
t_en: Ahem! Do pardon my impoliteness. I am Arturo, the acting dean of Paperfold University. It's my pleasure to meet the Nameless from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 你认识我？
t_en: You know me?

t_ch: 当然，匹诺康尼的股东亲自光临鄙校，我这个校长怎能一无所知？
t_en: Of course! When a shareholder of Penacony personally visits my school, how could I, as dean, be caught unawares?

t_ch: 你之前跑哪去了？
t_en: Where were you before?

t_ch: 这个…说来惭愧，我遇到了一些意外——
t_en: Well... I'm ashamed to say that I had run into some trouble...

t_ch: 你不会也是猴子吧？
t_en: Don't tell me that you're a monkey too.

t_ch: 怎么可能？我这辈子除了调皮捣蛋的差生，最讨厌的就是猴子！
t_en: Absolutely not! Apart from unruly underachievers, the only things that I hate the most in my entire lifetime are monkeys!

t_ch: 之前我本来准备亲自接待各位，结果鸢尾花家系送来的那几台教学器材突然失控，给我抓了起来！
t_en: I was preparing to welcome all of you myself. However, the teaching devices that the Iris Family sent over suddenly went rogue and seized me!

t_ch: 那群家伙把我五花大绑，还拿好几根香蕉堵上了我的嘴…唉，我再也不想吃香蕉了。
t_en: Those blasted devices tied my hands behind my back and stuffed my mouth with bananas... Ugh, I'll never eat another banana ever again.

t_ch: 你不在的时候发生了很多事…
t_en: Many things happened while you weren't around...

t_ch: 我知道，模因病毒，对吧？
t_en: So I've heard. You're referring to the memetic virus, right?

t_ch: 在梦境里，那东西比粘在鞋底的牛皮糖还要难缠。若不是家族和诸位贵客及时出手，后果将不堪设想。
t_en: In the Dreamscape, that thing is even more difficult to get rid of than gum on the bottom of your shoe. I can't imagine what would have happened if it were not for you and The Family.

t_ch: 听起来可真惨…
t_en: Sounds terrible...

t_ch: 嗐，和这所学校刚经历过的危机相比，我个人的苦难又算得上什么呢？该死的模因病毒！
t_en: Eh, my suffering is inconsequential when compared to the crisis that this university was put through. That darned memetic virus!

t_ch: 在梦境里，那东西比粘在鞋底的牛皮糖还要难缠。若不是家族和诸位贵客及时出手，后果将不堪设想。
t_en: In the Dreamscape, that thing is even more difficult to get rid of than gum on the bottom of your shoe. I can't imagine what would have happened if it were not for you and The Family.

t_ch: 是不是以后上不了蕉师的课了？
t_en: So, we won't be able to attend a BananAdvisor's class ever again?

t_ch: 孩子，如果你经历过我们折纸大学真正的课堂，就绝对不会想认猴子当老师！
t_en: My child, if you were to just experience an actual class from Paperfold University, you would have never thought of those monkeys as real teachers!

t_ch: 何况它们带来了可怕的模因病毒…在梦境里，那东西比粘在鞋底的牛皮糖还要难缠。若不是家族和诸位贵客及时出手，后果将不堪设想。
t_en: And they brought the terrifying memetic virus along with them... In the Dreamscape, that thing is even more difficult to get rid of than gum on the bottom of your shoe. I can't imagine what would have happened if it were not for you and The Family.

t_ch: 我回到学校之后，马上组织学校领导开展了一场反思大会，得出了一个结论——之所以模因病毒会趁虚而入，就是因为这届学生太散漫了！
t_en: Upon returning to the university, I immediately gathered the heads of the school and conducted a meeting of retrospection. Ultimately, we came to a conclusion: The memetic virus was able to slip into the campus because the current cohort of students is too undisciplined!

t_ch: 遥想当初我毕业那届学生，就业率可是百分之百。两千来号人没有一个无所事事的，全都成为了宇宙中的精英。
t_en: When I think about the class of students that I graduated with, I recall that the employment rate back then was a hundred percent! Two thousand people and not a single of them loafed around all day! And they all became the universe's elites!

t_ch: 再看现在的学生呢？就业率连一半都不到！还有的干脆离开了匹诺康尼，肥水全流了外人田！
t_en: As for the students now? Why, less than half of them are employed! Some even left Penacony altogether in a severe case of brain drain!

t_ch: 现在的学生喜欢的不是知识，而是一些乱七八糟的亚文化——这些东西都是滋生模因病毒的土壤！就算没有那蕉师，他们早晚也会被什么苹果师、鸭梨师给带坏！
t_en: Students nowadays aren't interested in knowledge anymore. Instead, they like all sorts of strange subcultures, which act as the perfect fodder for breeding memetic viruses! Even if those BananAdvisors didn't exist, the students would still be led astray by some apple or pear advisors sooner or later!

t_ch: <size=28>因此我决定，拟定<unbreak>88</unbreak>条新校规，好好整治一下这股歪风邪气！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Therefore, in order to clean up such toxic influences once and for all, I have decided to draft <unbreak>88</unbreak> new school rules!</size>

t_ch: 从明天起，学校实行全封闭式管理！不许私自外出、与校外人士厮混；学生必须参加晨跑，还得早中晚各拍一张宿舍照片发到辅导群里！
t_en: From tomorrow onward, this school shall be managed as an enclosed institution! No student shall be permitted to leave the university and fool around with outsiders. Furthermore, not only will students be required to participate in morning runs, but they'll also have to send a photo of the dormitory to the counseling group chat every morning, afternoon, and night!

t_ch: 午休从两小时缩短为三十分钟；课堂签到从声纹验证改为扫脸、指纹和采血三重验证，杜绝代签、漏签；晚自习必须自愿出席……
t_en: Afternoon breaks will be shortened from two hours to thirty minutes. Class attendance shall no longer be taken via voiceprint. A three-factor authentication process that includes facial scanning, fingerprint recognition, and blood sample verification will be used instead. Failing to take one's own attendance or taking attendance on behalf of others is strictly prohibited. Students must also voluntarily attend nightly self-study sessions...

t_ch: 有点太激进了…
t_en: These measures sound quite extreme...

t_ch: 激进？现在的学生一个个都那么有主意，必须出重拳！
t_en: Extreme? Since students nowadays have so many schemes up their sleeves, I have no other choice but to put my foot down!

t_ch: 就这？太保守了！
t_en: That's all? Your measures do not go far enough!

t_ch: …啊？我还担心太激进了，没想到你还觉得不够…怪不得你能成为匹诺康尼的股东，果然对自己够狠才能成功。
t_en: What...? I was somewhat worried that my measures were too extreme. I didn't expect you to say that they are not enough... No wonder you were able to become a shareholder of Penacony. Indeed, one must be hard on themselves in order to become successful.

t_ch: 不过大多数学生没你这种觉悟，光是这些规定就足够让他们掉一层皮，然后脱胎换骨了！
t_en: However, the vast majority of students aren't as enlightened as you are. These new rules alone are enough to make them cast off their old selves and be born anew.

t_ch: 你染上「秩序」了？
t_en: Have you been infected by the Order?

t_ch: 秩、秩序？这…这话可不能乱说啊！这些规定是为了让这群学生更加优秀，我校的老师领导们也要为此付出更多努力，这是多么无私的「同谐」精神啊！
t_en: O—Order? That's not something to joke about! These rules will allow the current batch of students to excel. The teachers and academic leaders will also spare no effort in accomplishing this goal. Doesn't this plan exemplify the selfless ethos of Harmony!?

t_ch: 学生就该专心学习，别一天到晚净想些没用的！这都是为了折纸大学——不，是为了匹诺康尼——不，是为了宇宙的未来！
t_en: Students must focus on their studies and stop thinking about useless stuff day in, day out! Everything that I'm doing is for Paperfold University, the whole of Penacony, and even the future of the universe!

t_ch: 阿图罗先生重新陷入了歇斯底里的状态，把待客的礼节抛到了脑后……
t_en: Mr. Arturo sinks into hysteria once more and completely forgets about the etiquette of hospitality...

t_ch: 一天到晚净想这些没用的——学生就该好好学习，天天向上！
t_en: Thinking about useless stuff day in, day out... No, students should focus on their studies and their self-improvement!

t_ch: 随着钟表的指针拨动，阿图罗先生止住动作、陷入了思考。他头顶的天环仿佛都黯淡了几分。
t_en: As the clock's hands tick forward, Mr. Arturo stops what he's doing and sinks into thought, causing the halo above his head to dim slightly.
Phew...

t_ch: 冷静下来了？
t_en: Have you calmed down now?

t_ch: …对。就在刚才，我意识到修改校规来得太慢了——不是说不修改，但在那之前，我得好好敲打几个学生。
t_en: Yes... I've just realized that changing the school rules will take too long. I'm still intent on implementing changes, mind you, but before that, I must knock some sense into a few students' minds.

t_ch: 毕业季就快到了，他们是我最寄予厚望、也最放心不下的孩子。我想拜托你替我帮他们带几句话。
t_en: Graduation season is right around the corner and these kids are the ones whom I have the highest of hopes for. Yet, they're also the ones that I'm worried about the most. Please, can you speak to them on my behalf?

t_ch: 行啊。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 很好，你的主人翁精神让我欣慰。要是每个学生都像你这么有担当，匹诺康尼肯定比现在还优秀好几倍。
t_en: Thank you, I'm heartened by your sense of commitment. If every student was as responsible as you are, Penacony would be a much more splendid place than it is now.

t_ch: 为啥是我？
t_en: Why me?

t_ch: 你们星穹列车是匹诺康尼的股东，学生就业率下降的话，匹诺康尼的股价也会受到影响——你肯定不希望这样的事情发生吧？
t_en: Since the Astral Express is one of Penacony's shareholders, if the employment rate of students drops, Penacony's stock price will be affected as well. Surely you don't wish for such a scenario to occur, do you?

t_ch: 首先要找的是谐乐学院的<color=#dbc291ff>哈里森</color>。那个老小子延毕了这么多年，是时候挪窝了——懂我意思吧？
t_en: Firstly, you must find <color=#dbc291ff>Harrison</color>, a student at Charmony Academy. Having delayed his graduation for so many years, it's time for that old bloke to head out into the world... if you catch my drift?

t_ch: 他的经历固然可惜，但人总是要向前看的。让他去找个工作，然后培养新的爱好吧。他的人生已经不剩多少可以浪费了。
t_en: While he has had some truly regrettable experiences, one must always look forward. Convince him to look for a job and nurture a new hobby. After all, there's little of his life left to waste.

t_ch: 然后是财富学院的<color=#dbc291ff>查德</color>。那小子自称什么…折纸大学派对王？哼，说得好听，不就是不务正业？
t_en: After that, find <color=#dbc291ff>Chad</color> from Fortune Academy, the one who proclaims himself to be... Paperfold University's "Party King," I believe? Hah, by giving himself that ridiculous title, isn't he just admitting how negligent he is of his studies?

t_ch: 他家里人多次打点过我，希望帮他指条明路。我亲自把他的简历送到了奥帝购物中心的人力部，结果他连笔试都没过——指了明路也得肯走才行啊！
t_en: His family members have contacted me numerous times before, always pleading with me to guide him onto the right path. Thereafter, I personally sent his resume to Oti Mall's HR department... only for him to fail the written test! I say, even if I'm able to point out a path forward, it matters not if the student in question refuses to follow my direction!

t_ch: 好在他距离毕业还有几年。这段时间内，务必让他搞清楚自己该做什么。机会只给有准备的人。
t_en: Thankfully, he's still a few years from graduating. Before he does so, it's imperative for us to make him realize what he should be doing with his life. After all, chance favors the prepared.

t_ch: 还有财富学院的主席<color=#dbc291ff>尚恩</color>。他是我遇到过最有潜力的学生，我本来想引荐他加入苜蓿草家系，但这小子居然接了星际和平公司的Offer！
t_en: And then there's <color=#dbc291ff>Shawn</color>, the president of Fortune Academy and the most promising student I've ever met. I was originally going to recommend him to join the Alfalfa Family. However, this kid accepted an offer from the IPC instead!

t_ch: 凭他的天赋，在匹诺康尼肯定能闯出一片天，但跑到星际和平公司那种人吃人的地方，给那些没有理想的老东西当奴隶——
t_en: Given how talented he is, he would have definitely been able to make a name for himself in Penacony. Yet, he chose to join a dog-eat-dog company like the IPC and serve as a workhorse for those unambitious squares...

t_ch: 你就让他好好想想，作为折纸大学的一份子，究竟是为了什么而读书？匹诺康尼重点培养他，是希望他能留下来建设同谐的美好梦境，不是给别人做嫁衣！
t_en: Ask him to think through his decision again, about what drives him to learn as a student at Paperfold University. Penacony has nurtured him so intently in the hopes that he will stay and build Harmony's wonderful Dreamscape, not for him to turn around and work for someone else!

t_ch: 阿图罗的脸憋得和血色刺蕉一样红，这位老教育家似乎说动了情，恨铁不成钢之心溢于言表。
t_en: Arturo's face has turned as red as a bloodthorn banana. It seems that this experienced educator has worked himself into a tizzy, his disappointment in the students written all over his face.

t_ch: 唉，就这样吧，一个个都不让人省心…我的话他们已经听不进去了。但凡事总得试试，说不定你能让他们迷途知返呢？
t_en: *sigh* That should do it for now. I'm always worried about these students... yet they don't seem to heed my advice anymore. Nevertheless, you might be able to make them see the error of their ways. It's worth a shot, right?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>哈里森</color>、<color=#dbc291ff>查德</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>尚恩</color>。这三个孩子，根本听不进我的话…也许你能让他们迷途知返？
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Harrison</color>, <color=#dbc291ff>Chad</color>, and <color=#dbc291ff>Shawn</color>, three obstinate kids who don't heed my advice... Perhaps you will be able to make them see the error of their ways.

t_ch: 校长有话托我带给你…
t_en: The dean has asked me to relay some words to you on his behalf...

t_ch: 行，他说了什么？
t_en: Go on, what did he say?

t_ch: 你转达了阿图罗先生给哈里森的嘱托——不要浪费生命。
t_en: You relay Mr. Arturo's advice to Harrison: Don't waste your life away.

t_ch: 哈、哈哈哈…这老小子，真是一点都不让人意外。
t_en: Ha... Hahaha... This old man is so predictable.

t_ch: 自打我们认识起，他就一直催我毕业、找工作。除了这个，我再没听他吐出半句有用的话！
t_en: From the moment we met, he has nagged me about graduating and finding a job. Apart from these, I've not heard a single piece of useful advice that has come out of his mouth!

t_ch: 人活一辈子到底是为了什么，工作吗？谁说不工作就不能活着？他越催我，我就越想不毕业！
t_en: What do humans live for? Work? Who says that a person who doesn't work can't survive just as well? The more he pesters me, the less I wish to graduate!

t_ch: 要不还是找个工作吧？
t_en: Perhaps you should still look for a job?

t_ch: 我都这个年纪了，如果要找工作，就只能从事音乐以外的行业…
t_en: At my age, if I really were to search for a job, I'd have to look for one that's not related to music...

t_ch: 比起做那些痛苦的差事，我宁愿窝在家里躺平——但这俩选择都不如继续上学。
t_en: Instead of wearing myself down with tedious trivialities, I'd rather laze about at home... Yet, neither of these choices beats continual education.

t_ch: 为什么不毕业呢？
t_en: Why don't you wish to graduate?

t_ch: 毕了业又如何？现在的我已经没机会投身音乐行业了，不如在学校里听年轻人唱歌。
t_en: What's so good about graduating? At my age, there are no longer any opportunities for me to work in the music industry. In that case, I'd rather stay in school and listen to the youngsters' singing.

t_ch: 都说学校是座象牙塔，我同意。不过时间久了，我发现象牙塔也有象牙塔的好处——你不用担心跟外界同流合污。
t_en: Some say that academia is an ivory tower, and I agree with that sentiment. However, as time passed, I realized that ivory towers have their own advantages: One who lives in an ivory tower need not worry about wallowing in the filth that is the outside world.

t_ch: 这片宇宙是黑暗的。天真的学生踏入其中，就像水滴被投入染缸。跟一群脏人待在一起，不干脏事就活不下去。
t_en: The universe is dark. Naive students step into it like water being poured into a vat of dye. Those who hang out with dirty people must also do dirty things for survival.

t_ch: 未免有些极端了吧…
t_en: That's quite an extreme perspective...

t_ch: 极端？我那个爹，当初口口声声说要支持我实现梦想，结果只是为了让我做企业代言人，好帮他那些见不得人的生意打掩护！
t_en: Extreme? Back then, my dad kept saying that he would support my dream. However, he was just paying lip service to me in order to convince me to take on the role of enterprise ambassador and help him cover up all of his shady businesses that couldn't see the light of day!

t_ch: 说得好像你见过似的…
t_en: You speak as though you've personally witnessed such events before...

t_ch: 没见过？我甚至被自己的亲爹骗过！
t_en: Witnessed? No, worse than that, I've even been lied to by my own dad before!

t_ch: 当初他口口声说要支持我实现梦想，结果只是为了让我做企业代言人，好帮他那些见不得人的生意打掩护！
t_en: Back then, he kept saying that he would support my dream. However, he was just paying lip service to me in order to convince me to take on the role of enterprise ambassador and help him cover up all of his shady businesses that couldn't see the light of day!

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 我可不是瞎掰。我那个爹，当初口口声声说要支持我实现梦想，结果只是为了让我做企业代言人，好帮他那些见不得人的生意打掩护！
t_en: I'm not making things up. Back then, my dad kept saying that he would support my dream. However, he was just paying lip service to me in order to convince me to take on the role of enterprise ambassador and help him cover up all of his shady businesses that couldn't see the light of day!

t_ch: 后来我从星际和平网络了解到，这种事压根算不上什么新闻——有钱人的嘴脸，全宇宙都一个样。
t_en: Later, thanks to the Interastral Peace Network, I realized that such events aren't even considered news at all: Wealthy people act the same, regardless of where they are in the universe.

t_ch: 要么当赚黑心钱的老板，要么给赚黑心钱的老板打工。只有最偏远的蛮荒星球才能躲过金钱的浸染…这就是宇宙的真相。
t_en: It's either you become a boss who rakes in dirty money, or you work for a boss like that. The corruption of wealth can only be avoided by fleeing to the remotest of barren planets... This is but a universal truth.

t_ch: 阿图罗倒是口口声声让我向前看，可前面等着我的又是什么？毕了业融入社会就能让我的生活有意义吗？
t_en: Arturo keeps telling me to look forward, but is there anything that's waiting for me at the other end? If I graduate and integrate into society, will my life suddenly have meaning?

t_ch: 你就告诉他…我是快入土了没错。但我宁可带着梦想去死，也不愿像个行尸走肉一样活着！这是我最后的反抗！
t_en: You can tell him that while I have one foot in the grave, I'd rather die with my dreams intact than live like a walking corpse! This is my final rebellion!

t_ch: 校长有话托我带给你…
t_en: The dean has asked me to relay some words to you on his behalf...

t_ch: 校长？呃，有股不祥的预感。
t_en: The dean? Um... I've got a bad feeling about this.

t_ch: 你转达了阿图罗先生给查德的嘱托——不要玩物丧志。
t_en: You relay Mr. Arturo's advice to Chad: Constant play saps one's aspirations away.

t_ch: …唉，校长啊校长，为什么偏偏要让我停办派对去找工作…这可真是太难为我了。
t_en: *sigh* Is the dean really asking me to stop partying and look for a job? That would be nearly impossible for me to accomplish.

t_ch: 派对瘾太大了？
t_en: Because you're addicted to partying?

t_ch: 不是！朋友，你有所不知，如今匹诺康尼正在面临前所未有的困境…简单来说，就是找不到工作。
t_en: No! Friend, you might not know about this, but Penacony is currently in the midst of an unprecedented crisis... To put it simply, there are barely any jobs available.

t_ch: 工作太难找了？
t_en: Because it's too difficult to find a job?

t_ch: 可不是嘛！朋友，你有所不知，如今匹诺康尼正在面临前所未有的困境…简单来说，就是找不到工作。
t_en: That's right! Friend, you might not know about this, but Penacony is currently in the midst of an unprecedented crisis... To put it simply, there are barely any jobs available.

t_ch: 没法站着把钱挣了？
t_en: Can't just make money standing around anymore?

t_ch: 这都什么跟什么…朋友，你有所不知，如今匹诺康尼正在面临前所未有的困境。简单来说，就是找不到工作。
t_en: Huh? What are you talking about... Anyway, friend, you might not know about this, but Penacony is currently in the midst of an unprecedented crisis. To put it simply, there are barely any jobs available.

t_ch: 这可不是我们不努力，我的分数也不低。但课上教的跟工作要用的，根本不是一回事！
t_en: It's not because we're not hardworking enough. After all, my grades are pretty decent, if I say so myself. However, the stuff that we're being taught won't even be useful in the workplace!

t_ch: 而且最近大环境变化得实在太快。自从星际和平公司入驻之后，全宇宙的能人都往这边挤，说是什么「要让美梦再次多彩」…美梦多没多彩我不知道，岗位竞争倒是越来越激烈了。
t_en: Furthermore, the world nowadays is changing at a rapid pace. Ever since the IPC established itself in Penacony, all manner of talented people from across the universe are gathering here and crowding up the place, claiming that they wish to "make dreams colorful again." While I can't tell you how colorful the dreams are at present, the fact remains that the fight for jobs has become more and more intense.

t_ch: 那些外来者都是一等一的精英，业务能力强就算了，薪资要的还低——据说里面有不少被其他星球「优化」掉的人才，性价比爆了我们这些应届生几条街！
t_en: Those outsiders are truly the cream of the crop. Apart from being incredibly competent, they're asking for lower wages as well. I've even heard that there are talents who were the victims of downsizing on other planets among them. Their cost-performance ratio must be through the roof compared to the fresh graduates from our school!

t_ch: 之前校长发内推时，我还想着从基层做起、慢慢锻炼，就去应聘了奥帝购物中心的销售助理……
t_en: Some time ago, when the dean was sending out internal recommendations, I was thinking that I could start at the bottom and slowly work my way up, so I went to apply for a sales assistant position at Oti Mall...

t_ch: 就这么个薪资最低的岗位，你猜有多少人跟我抢？十万人！简历多到要用人工智能来审核，我连面试都没进去就被刷掉了。
t_en: Guess how many people were also vying for this low-paying position. One hundred thousand! Oti Mall received so many resumes that AI had to be used to screen them. I was written off even before the interview stage.

t_ch: 我一看，好哇，要是还抱着这条赛道不松手，我毕业之后非得被赶出匹诺康尼不可——我爹肯定不允许我在家啃老，不然再多财富也有花光的一天。
t_en: I was absolutely stunned by this. If I keep pursuing the same path with blind persistence, I would definitely be chased out of Penacony upon graduation. After all, my dad would never let me leech off the family, otherwise we'll eventually run out of money, regardless of how much wealth we started with.

t_ch: 既然这样，还不如早点转型，另谋出路。至少派对行业在匹诺康尼算是个蓝海，不比跟那群逐梦客内卷强多了？
t_en: In that case, I might as well pivot early and look for greener pastures elsewhere. At least the party sector in Penacony still has tons of employment opportunities. Wouldn't pursuing this be more promising than joining the rat race alongside the other Dreamchasers?

t_ch: 感觉你说的有些道理…
t_en: You sound rather sensible...

t_ch: 感觉你说的有些偏颇…
t_en: You sound rather biased...

t_ch: 感觉你说了…一些话。
t_en: You... made a sound?

t_ch: 唉，反正我也不指望能说服校长。他老了，老人有自己的一套逻辑。但要论派对行业的发展前景，他未必有我懂。
t_en: *sigh* I'm pretty certain that I can't convince the dean anyway. He's quite old, and old people have their own way of thinking. However, when it comes to the future prospects of the party industry, he might not be as knowledgeable about that as I am.

t_ch: 到时候你就跟他说：「校长的好意，查德心领了。可查德实在没有别的特长，适应不了竞争激烈的社会，只能凭自己的意愿碰碰运气。母校的教诲，我时刻铭记在心。」
t_en: When you see him again, you can tell him this, "I understand your good intentions. However, since I don't excel at anything else and can't adapt to an intensely competitive society, I can only try my luck as I see fit. As for the things that I learned at my alma mater, I will always bear them in my heart."

t_ch: 希望这能让他好受一些吧……
t_en: I hope that these words of mine will make him feel slightly better...

t_ch: 校长有话托我带给你…
t_en: The dean has asked me to relay some words to you on his behalf...

t_ch: 哦？难道他打算为我发一份奖学金？居然现在才想起来，真是贵人多忘事。
t_en: Oh? Does he intend to award a scholarship to me? I can't believe I'm only just remembering this. It seems that great wits do indeed have short memories.

t_ch: 你转达了阿图罗先生给尚恩的嘱托——不要背井离乡。
t_en: You relay Mr. Arturo's advice to Shawn: Don't turn your back on your home planet.

t_ch: 啧…阿图罗先生，居然也有大智若愚的时候。他对商业、对整个宇宙运作规律的理解，都太肤浅了。我对他的评价下降了一些。
t_en: Tsk... It seems that even the great Mr. Arturo can have moments of folly as well. His understanding of commerce and the way the universe works is too superficial. After hearing these words, I actually think less of him than before.

t_ch: 居然说什么我应该留下来建设匹诺康尼…都这年头了，还一点宇宙化思维都没有。当初要是听他的，我可拿不到战略投资部的offer！
t_en: I can't believe he's suggesting that I should stay behind and develop Penacony... For someone as advanced in age as he is, I'm surprised how lacking he is in cosmic thinking. If I listened to him back then, I would have never received the Strategic Investment Department's offer!

t_ch: 什么是宇宙化思维？
t_en: What is cosmic thinking?

t_ch: 不错，很会抓重点——你想啊，匹诺康尼如今几十亿住民，都是土生土长的本地人吗？当年的囚犯们这么能生？
t_en: Not bad, at least you are able to grasp the important concept. Think about it: Do you believe that billions of people that reside in Penacony today have been born and bred here? Surely you don't think the prisoners who once inhabited this place had so many offsprings?

t_ch: 建设家乡不好吗？
t_en: Is staying back and developing your home planet not a good thing?

t_ch: 家乡？我爷爷还住在新伦蒂尼恩呢。匹诺康尼如今几十亿住民，都是土生土长的本地人吗？当年的囚犯们这么能生？
t_en: My home planet? My grandpa is still living on New Londinium! Do you believe that billions of people that reside in Penacony today have been born and bred here? Surely you don't think the prisoners who once inhabited this place had so many offsprings?

t_ch: 我也想要offer！
t_en: I want an offer too!

t_ch: 那只能靠你自己努力…别打岔！
t_en: Then you'd better work hard for it... Anyway, don't interrupt me!

t_ch: 我说到哪儿了…哦对，那老头不想我出去闯荡。呵，想想都好笑，匹诺康尼如今几十亿住民，都是土生土长的本地人吗？当年的囚犯们这么能生？
t_en: Now, where was I... Oh right, that old fogey doesn't want me to venture out into the universe. Heh, it's quite funny in retrospect: Do you believe that billions of people that reside in Penacony today have been born and bred here? Surely you don't think the prisoners who once inhabited this place had so many offsprings?

t_ch: 说白了，星际迁居本就是常态，人要往高处走，这没啥不道德。庇尔波因特是发达行星，机会比匹诺康尼多，我去那里发展，跟逐梦客来这儿有什么区别？
t_en: To put it bluntly, interstellar migration has always been the norm. There's nothing unethical about humanity's wish to soar higher. Since Pier Point is a developed planet, there are naturally more opportunities to be found there than at Penacony. How is my wish to advance my career there any different from the Dreamchasers who come here for a better life?

t_ch: 在财富学院，能赚到钱才是硬道理。当今这寰宇，有哪个大厂比得过星际和平公司？我都跟业界大拿喝咖啡了，还不够给母校争光？
t_en: A universal truth that we learned at Fortune Academy was to accumulate wealth by any means necessary. And when we look at the world today, is there any company that's bigger than the IPC? I'll be rubbing shoulders with industry leaders, but that's still not enough to bring glory to my alma mater?

t_ch: 嗐，话不投机半句多，今天这些言论还请不要转告校长。我只想老老实实毕业，然后奔向我的光明未来。
t_en: *sigh* I don't know what else I can say to him. As for what I have told you today, please keep it from the dean. I just want to graduate peacefully and then chase after my glorious future.

t_ch: …兴许在庇尔波因特升任P<unbreak>40</unbreak>、把家人接去享福之后，我会给他寄点星际特产回来吧，哈哈！
t_en: Maybe I'll have some interstellar specialties delivered to him once I've been promoted to P<unbreak>40</unbreak> and I can move my family to live comfortably together with me in Pier Point. Haha!

t_ch: 咕咕，同学，请稍作留步——房里的「小鸟」似乎遇到了麻烦。
t_en: Hoot, dear student, please wait a moment. The "fledgling" in this room seems to be in a spot of trouble.

t_ch: 老鸟已经在外面观察了好一阵子。他的心绪虽然被你拨动，但心中的折痕却并未被抚平。
t_en: I've been observing for a while from outside. You may have stirred his emotions, but the creases in his heart have yet to be smoothed out.

t_ch: 咕咕…依老鸟看，他就像钟表小子第<unbreak>3210</unbreak>集里被藤壶爬满身躯的鲸鱼叔叔，已经负重累累，把脑子都累昏了。
t_en: Hoot, the way I see it, he's just like Uncle Whale from Episode <unbreak>3210</unbreak> of Clockie — overburdened by the barnacles all over his body to the point where his mind has grown muddled and weary.

t_ch: 为学生解惑是老师的责任，看来老鸟必须站出来说几句话、提点提点他才行。
t_en: It's a teacher's responsibility to help students through difficult problems, so it seems I must step forward to say a few words and point him in the right direction.

t_ch: 他是当今校长…
t_en: He's the current dean...

t_ch: 咕咕——老鸟当然知道，他现在在帮老鸟照看着这片树林，是其他小鸟们的大家长。
t_en: Hoot, I know that. He's helping me look after this forest and serves as a guardian to the other fledglings.

t_ch: 不过，树林里每一只小鸟的成长老鸟都看在眼里——他曾经也是一只迷茫的小鸟，现在看来依然需要老鸟的呵护。
t_en: However, I also keep watch over every fledglings' growth in this forest. He was once a confused fledgling too, and it seems he still needs some care from this old bird.

t_ch: 可其他人看不到你…
t_en: But other people can't see you...

t_ch: 咕咕…说得是呢，看来老鸟有些太过心急了。
t_en: Hoot, you're right... It seems I got ahead of myself.

t_ch: 可否请你拿来纸笔，替老鸟写一封口述信，转交给他？
t_en: Could you bring over a pen and piece of paper so you can write down what I say and deliver it to him?

t_ch: 好！
t_en: Sure!

t_ch: 那老鸟要开始念了。可能会有些慢，请同学务必保持耐心……
t_en: Then I'll start dictating. I may be a bit slow, so please be patient with me...

t_ch: 不好！
t_en: No can do!

t_ch: 咕咕——你是一个热心肠的孩子，老鸟相信你只是在开玩笑。就算是认真的，也权当给老鸟这只<color=#dbc291ff>初代校长</color>一个面子，好吗？
t_en: Hoot, you're a warm-hearted child, so I trust you were just joking. But if you were serious, could you see it as doing a favor for the <color=#dbc291ff>first dean</color> instead?

t_ch: 这番话着实有些让人难以拒绝。在某种情绪的推动下，你拿出了纸笔……
t_en: You find it difficult to refuse such a request. Driven by a certain emotion, you take out a pen and piece of paper...

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师的声音突然变得严肃，它收起了可爱的声音与「咕咕」的口癖，认真地向你传达他的心意……
t_en: Professor Owl's voice suddenly becomes solemn, gone is the cuteness and his "hoot" catchphrase as he earnestly conveys his feelings to you...

t_ch: 你很少有机会起草这样正规的文书，但意外完成得还不错——除了个别字存在涂抹痕迹之外。
t_en: You rarely have the chance to draft up a proper correspondence like this, but it turned out surprisingly well, apart from the smudges on certain letters.

t_ch: …以上，希望能解开那只小鸟的心结。老鸟就先回大树了，还有其他小鸟也需要照顾咕。
t_en: ...That's all. I hope this will help the fledgling with his troubles. I'll return to the Great Tree now. There are other fledglings in need of my care too, hoot.

t_ch: ——来自罗盘号的同学，聪明如你，一定能找到合适的机会交出这封信咕。
t_en: ...A brilliant student from The Compass like you will surely find a suitable opportunity to deliver this letter, hoot.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>哈里森</color>、<color=#dbc291ff>查德</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>尚恩</color>。这三个孩子，根本听不进我的话…也许你能让他们迷途知返？
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Harrison</color>, <color=#dbc291ff>Chad</color>, and <color=#dbc291ff>Shawn</color>, three obstinate kids who don't heed my advice... Perhaps you will be able to make them see the error of their ways.

t_ch: 我传达了你的寄语…
t_en: I've relayed your words...

t_ch: 是吗，他们什么反应？
t_en: Is that so. How did they react?

t_ch: 你向阿图罗先生如实复述了三位学生的回答，包括那些摆烂充满攻击性的语句。
t_en: You relay to Mr. Arturo the three students' answers as they were presented to you, including the phrasings where they've given up and the ones that felt like personal attacks.

t_ch: 哼，不出所料。类似的言论我在其他学生那里也听到过一些…真搞不懂现在的年轻人。
t_en: Hmph, as I expected. I've heard similar views from my other students. I just don't understand young people nowadays...

t_ch: 这三个家伙我是爱莫能助了，但愿我开的猛药可以帮到之后的学生——能救一个算一个。
t_en: There's nothing more I can do to help those three. I only hope my stricter regimen will help future students — even if I can only save them one at a time.

t_ch: 既然如此，就从下个月开始试行全封闭式管理好了——
t_en: If this is the case, I'll try managing the school as an enclosed institution from next month.

t_ch: 唉，这些学生也是费尽千辛万苦才考进这所学校来的，怎么就对这机会一点都不珍惜？
t_en: *sigh* These students spent a lot of time and effort to get into this school, so why don't they cherish this opportunity at all?

t_ch: 或许等到将来的某天，他们回想自己年轻时做过的这些错事，会得出不一样的感悟…可那时后悔也晚了。
t_en: Perhaps they'll look back on the errors of their youth some day in the future and come to a different realization... Though it'll be far too late for regrets by then.

t_ch: 感觉这间屋子好久没收拾了…你还有什么事吗？
t_en: This room feels overdue for a tidying... Did you need anything else?

t_ch: 这里有给你的信。
t_en: I have a letter for you.

t_ch: 给我的信？谁会挑这个时候……
t_en: A letter for me? Why now and from who?

t_ch: 在接过信封的一瞬，阿图罗先生愣住了，因为信封上画着一只歪歪扭扭的折纸鸟——你花了些力气才尽可能把它画得像鸟。
t_en: Mr. Arturo looks stunned the moment he accepts the envelope. There is a crooked drawing of an origami bird on the envelope — a drawing you spent some effort into making resemble a bird as much as possible.

t_ch: 仔细看去，这只鸟与当今折纸大学的校徽颇为相似，却又有几处明显的不同。只有打开校长室内某个尘封的抽屉才能找到这图案的原型……
t_en: On closer inspection, this bird looks quite similar to the current crest of Paperfold University, yet it also has some distinct differences. Only by opening a certain dusty drawer in the Dean's Office will one be able to find the original concept for this design.

t_ch: 这是一版未被选用的校徽提案。能知道它的要么是历代校长，要么是早在建校之初就已抵达匹诺康尼的某位名宿…亦或两者都是。
t_en: This is a proposed version of the university crest that went unused. Only two types of individuals would know of it: Someone who is or was once a dean, or someone of renown who had already arrived in Penacony in the early days of the university's construction, or... someone who is both.

t_ch: 这，这是……
t_en: I—Is this...

t_ch: 来不及问这图案是谁告诉你的，阿图罗先生立即拆开了信封，仿佛刚踏入大学的新生般急切地读了起来。
t_en: Without even waiting to ask who told you about the design, Mr. Arturo immediately opens the envelope and eagerly reads its contents like a freshman on his first day of university.

t_ch: 你好，亲爱的阿图罗小鸟，请允许我这样称呼你。我是猫头鹰老师，一只总是在关注小鸟们健康成长的老鸟。
t_en: Hello, my dearest fledgling Arturo. I hope you'll permit me to refer to you as such. I'm Professor Owl, an old bird who has been closely watching the growth and health of my fledglings.

t_ch: 在你看来，折纸大学或许已经到了生死存亡的关头：学生们被名为散漫的阴云笼罩，抛弃了学校的优良传统，难以肩负匹诺康尼的重任。
t_en: Perhaps from where you're standing, Paperfold University has reached a crucial juncture of life and death: Your students are shrouded in a cloud of disorganization and lack of discipline. They've abandoned the school's fine traditions and are having a difficult time shouldering the responsibilities of Penacony.

t_ch: 过去发生在这学校的点点滴滴，老鸟一直看在眼里。关于如今学生们面临的问题，老鸟也一直在思考，现在是时候给出一些看法了。
t_en: I've observed every little thing that has happened in this school and have given a lot of thought to the problems the students are currently facing. And now it is time I express those thoughts.

t_ch: 老鸟执教时见识过不少带伤飞翔的孩子。有时我会帮那些小鸟一把，但更多时候我会选择沉默，等待他们靠自己的能力飞上天空。
t_en: When I was still teaching, I witnessed many children who flew with injured wings. Sometimes I would give those fledglings a hand, but more often, I would remain silent and wait until they were able to take to the skies by their own power.

t_ch: 毕竟，只有在年轻时多摔跤，长大后骨头才能更加牢固。倘若害怕错误，那便告别了进步。
t_en: After all, it is only by falling over many times in our youth that our bones can become stronger in adulthood. If we become afraid of making mistakes, we would only bid farewell to progress.

t_ch: 承认吧，即便羽翼未丰，你树上的小鸟也终究不是雏鸟了。我们规定要去做的事，于他们而言未必是最好的选择。
t_en: You have to admit, even though their feathers aren't fully developed yet, the fledglings in your tree are all ready to take flight. What we instruct them to do may not always be the best choice for them.

t_ch: 作为老人，我们需要按耐住自己想要伸手的心，为孩子们提供支持而非干涉。假以时日，他们身上的*蕉燥*自会痊愈。
t_en: As the elder, we must suppress our desire to reach out and instead offer the children support without meddling. With time, they will recover from their banananxiety.

t_ch: 可是…我们的承诺该怎么办？为学生解惑是老师的责任啊。
t_en: But... what about our commitment? It's a teacher's responsibility to help students through difficult problems.

t_ch: 阿图罗先生读得如此投入，情不自禁地问出了声。而「猫头鹰老师」仿佛对他的疑问早有预料，在信的下一段给出了回答：
t_en: Mr. Arturo is so absorbed in reading that he can't help but voice his confusion out loud. As if predicting his query, "Professor Owl" provides an answer in the next passage of his letter:

t_ch: 前面的路，终究要让他们自己走；往后的事，要由他们自己思考。他们已经可以为自己的选择负责了。
t_en: The road ahead is meant for them to walk alone. Similarly, only they can reflect on what will happen in their future. They're ready to be responsible for their own choices.

t_ch: 而且，哪怕短短十年的时间都足以让世界变一番模样，我们又怎能确定自己的处世经验会同样适用于年轻人呢？
t_en: Not to mention, ten years is enough time for the world to look completely different. How can we be sure our life experiences would still apply to the youths now?

t_ch: 时代曾经属于我们，后来属于你们，但终将属于他们。无论多么挂念，你我也没法替他们走向下一个琥珀纪。
t_en: The era that once belonged to us eventually became yours, and now it will soon become theirs. No matter how much you worry, neither you nor I can help them find their path into the next Amber Era.

t_ch: 我选择将未来交付给年轻人，阿图罗小鸟，也是你做出选择的时候了。
t_en: I choose to leave the future to the youths. So, young fledgling Arturo, it's also time for you to make your own decision.

t_ch: 老鸟不打算教你该如何去做。作为小鸟，你应该是自由的。只是下决定之前，务必记得我们的校训——
t_en: This old bird will not tell you what choice you should make. As a fledgling, you should be free. However, before you decide, always keep our school's motto in mind...

t_ch: 「美梦多彩，理想自由」。
t_en: "Color your dreams, free your ideals."

t_ch: 校长的表情变得相当复杂，里面蕴含着等量的喜悦与苦涩。
t_en: The dean has a complicated expression on his face, one that seems to contain equal amounts of joy and bitterness.

t_ch: 小鸟…我都这把年纪了，哪里还算得上什么小鸟呢？
t_en: Young fledgling... At this age, how can I still be considered a young fledgling?

t_ch: 也许在他看来…
t_en: Perhaps to him...

t_ch: 学院里的每个人都是小鸟。
t_en: Everyone in the academy can be considered young fledglings.

t_ch: 折纸大学还真是有一位爱操心的校长，居然还在关心着这所学校，明明都已经……
t_en: Paperfold University really does have a worrywart of a dean. He still cares so much about the school, even though he's already...

t_ch: 他说的对，我抓住那些学生的脚，生怕他们飞到沟里去，并把这当成是对他们的保护…或许反倒激起了不少人的叛逆心理吧。
t_en: But he's right. I've shackled the feet of the students in the name of protection, fearing that they would crash when they take flight... Perhaps this was what stirred up the rebellious attitude in so many of them.

t_ch: 信中的一些内容我仍持保留意见。不过校长有一点说进了我心里——时代。
t_en: Although I still have my reservations regarding some of the contents in the letter, what the dean said regarding the era got to me...

t_ch: 时代啊…它总在不断变化，而我已经是个老人了。很多事情我只是遵照惯例在做，并没有真的看透多少，但求无过罢了。
t_en: The times... are constantly changing, and I'm also no longer young. I've been simply following the convention and not giving much thought to things, only hoping to not make any mistakes.

t_ch: 即便还有些放心不下，我也确实该松松手、去歇一歇了…不然光是我这老腰就容易扛不住。
t_en: Even though I still can't help but worry, perhaps it's time that I let go of the reins and take a break... Otherwise, my poor back might not be able to take it for much longer.

t_ch: 你认识这封信的主人？
t_en: Do you know who this letter is from?

t_ch: 这封信的主人，就是折纸大学唯一的那位「校长」吧。
t_en: This letter can only be from the only true dean of Paperfold University.

t_ch: 他曾是每一位筑梦师的偶像，也是我们任教人员的榜样。即使他离开了这么久，折纸大学仍为他保留着正式校长的空位。
t_en: He was once the idol of every Dreamweaver. Even now, he is still the role model of us educators. Even after such a long time has passed, the official position of dean in Paperfold University is still left empty for him.

t_ch: 有人说他最后在偏远的梦境安度了晚年，也有人认为他遭遇了可怕的意外…但现在来看，这些猜测都不对，他只是把「未来」交给了小鸟们，然后飞回了自己的树林。
t_en: Some people say that he ended up living out his old age peacefully in a remote dreamscape, while others believe that he had met with a terrible accident... But now it seems like none of those theories are correct. He simply left the future to the fledglings before flying back to his own forest.

t_ch: 那校规还改吗？
t_en: Then are you still going to change the school rules?

t_ch: 先放一放吧。这种大事还是得等考虑清楚后再做决策。
t_en: I'll give it more thought. Such major changes have to be carefully considered before the final decision is made.

t_ch: …不过，关于严肃打卡纪律的那条新规必须推进下去。现在的学生出勤率实在太低了——至少一个学期来上一堂课吧！
t_en: ...However, the new rule regarding the strict enforcement of attendance taking has to be implemented. Nowadays, students barely attend any classes. They should at least turn up for one class each semester!

t_ch: 还有好多东西要批复…哦，是你啊。
t_en: There are still so many things waiting for me to approve... Oh, it's you.

t_ch: 阿图罗先生轻咳一声，换上了一副波澜不惊的面孔——其中藏着一丝难掩的尴尬。
t_en: Mr. Arturo coughs quietly and puts on a composed front, but a hint of embarrassment that he fails to hide betrays him.

t_ch: 人老了，有的时候就会控制不住情绪…也许是该找个接班人了？
t_en: As we get older, we sometimes fail to control our emotions... Perhaps it's about time that I look for a successor.

t_ch: 唉，腰又开始疼了。你说我一天到晚生这气图什么……
t_en: *sigh* My back is starting to hurt again. Tell me, of what use is this anger I feel every day...

t_ch: 啧…都说学校教的东西没用，我又何尝不清楚这些呢？
t_en: Tsk... People say that things taught in school are useless, but I'm well aware of that fact, too.

t_ch: 可是人活在世，终有一日要投入汪洋。我又没法赐他们一艘避浪的船，只能尽量让他们学会踩水，别被淹死就谢天谢地了。
t_en: Living in this world, everyone has to set sail into the vast world someday. Since I cannot offer them a boat to help them overcome the choppy waters, all I can do is to teach them how to stay afloat in hopes that they will not drown.

t_ch: 就算他们中有人能成功游到对岸，那也是他们自己受益，不是我——功成名就之后肯回头帮母校一把的又有几个？都不够我操心的！
t_en: Even if some of them manage to make it to the opposite shore, the one who has benefited will be them and not me... After achieving success, how many of them would even be willing to return and contribute to their alma mater? There's simply too many things I need to worry about!

t_ch: 唉呀呀，腰又开始疼了。你说我一天到晚生这气图什么……
t_en: *sigh*... My back is starting to hurt again. Tell me, of what use is this anger I feel every day...

t_ch: 真正的才华，学校根本教不出来……
t_en: True talent is not something that can be taught in schools...

t_ch: 唉…别说学生不学习了，就算是我，这一屋子书平时也没什么机会去翻动。想来还真是有些对不起集书的先辈。
t_en: *sigh* ...It's not only the students who don't study. Even with the collection of books in this room, I barely get a chance to flip through them. I'm starting to feel a little sorry for the predecessors who have painstakingly gathered the books.

t_ch: 这样的学习氛围，又怎么好潜下心来学知识呢？
t_en: With such a learning environment, how can anyone calm themselves and properly focus on learning?

t_ch: 倘若某天，折纸大学能诞生一位贤才，或许整个匹诺康尼都会迎来转机吧。
t_en: Perhaps someday in the future, if Paperfold University can produce a true talented genius, the situation in Penacony might take a turn for the better.

t_ch: 可惜，真正的才华，根本就不是从学校里教出来的……
t_en: It's such a pity that true talent is not something that can be taught in schools...

t_ch: 看他们饿不着肚子，我也就不担心了，哈哈！
t_en: But since I see that they're able to fend for themselves, there's nothing for me to worry about, haha!

t_ch: 哈，查德那小子给我发了个视频，说自己正在开拓什么「派对娱乐主播」行业，估计又是他自己造的词……
t_en: Ha, that brat Chad sent me a video saying that he's about to start a career as something called a "party and entertainment streamer." That sounds like a job title that he came up with himself...

t_ch: 别说，视频内容还真挺好笑。我怎么从来没想到让蛋糕吃人这么个点子呢！
t_en: But I have to say, his video is quite amusing. Why have I never come up with the idea of having a cake eat a person!?

t_ch: 他说只要假以时日，自己肯定能成为这个细分领域的头部主播，不负自己「折纸大学派对王」的头衔。
t_en: He says that given time, he'll definitely become the top streamer in this niche industry. That way, he'll truly live up to his title of "Paperfold University's Party King."

t_ch: 我倒是不指望他做到头部，能不饿肚子就行了——不过现在看来，我之前确实是有些担心过度咯，哈哈！
t_en: I don't expect him to reach the top of his field. I just want him to be able to feed himself. But now, it seems like I have indeed been worrying a little too much, haha!

t_ch: 我还想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to continue chatting with you.

t_ch: 说吧，正好我也歇歇腰。
t_en: Go ahead. It'll also be an opportunity for me to rest my back.

t_ch: 关于睡蕉小猴的后续…
t_en: About the aftermath of Slumbernana Monkey...

t_ch: 你在担心那件事？放心，模因病毒一经治愈，就不会再卷土重来了。
t_en: You're still worried about that? Don't worry. Once the memetic virus has been cured, there's no way that it'll be able to return.

t_ch: 家族已经用反模因手段清除了那些猴子的残留痕迹。至于更具体的来龙去脉，猎犬写了一份分锅用的报告……
t_en: The Family has already employed anti-memetic measures to remove the remaining trace of those monkeys. As for the specifics, it's all written in the report the Bloodhounds have prepared with regard to this incident...

t_ch: 虽说是机密，不过发给身为匹诺康尼股东的你倒也不算违规。自己看看吧。
t_en: Although it's meant to be confidential, sharing it with a shareholder of Penacony isn't considered a violation of the rules. Take a look for yourself.

t_ch: 当校长是什么感觉？
t_en: What is it like being the dean?

t_ch: 校长啊…听起来是不是像个大官？其实也没想象中那么自在。至少在折纸大学，校长能决定的东西不是很多。
t_en: The dean, huh... Although it sounds like a powerful position, it is not as unrestrained as you would imagine. At least in Paperfold University, there isn't much that the dean actually has authority over.

t_ch: 对上，各大家系都密切关注着折纸大学的发展，那些权贵可不是校长能得罪起的……
t_en: Above the dean, the various Lineages are closely monitoring the developments in Paperfold University. The rich and powerful are not people that the dean can offend...

t_ch: 对下，专业的师资总共就那么些，校长敢摆架子，教授就敢跳槽……
t_en: Beneath the dean, there are only so many professional teachers. The professors will leave if they are unhappy with anything...

t_ch: 更别说每天批不完的请示和拿不到的经费了，谁想谁头疼。
t_en: This is even before mentioning the endless mountain of requests waiting for my approval and the funding that always seems to be far from reach. It would give any person a major headache.

t_ch: 不过没办法，必须要有一位校长。要是谁有能力替我头疼，这把交椅让给他也无妨。
t_en: But there's no way around it. The position of dean is a necessary one. If anyone is capable of taking over this headache from me, I wouldn't mind handing my position over to them.

t_ch: 折纸大学都教些啥？
t_en: What does Paperfold University teach?

t_ch: 折纸大学总共有三大学院：由鸢尾花家系赞助的谐乐学院，由隐夜鸫家系赞助的筑梦学院，以及由苜蓿草家系赞助的财富学院。
t_en: There are three academies at Paperfold University: Charmony Academy sponsored by the Iris Family, Dreamweaver Academy sponsored by the Nightingale Family, and Fortune Academy sponsored by the Alfalfa Family.

t_ch: 筑梦学院是折纸大学最早开设的学院，也是建校之初唯一的学院，教学内容包括建筑学、城市规划、梦境内容设计等。
t_en: The first academy to be established at Paperfold University was Dreamweaver Academy. It was also the only academy when the university was founded. Subjects such as Architecture, Urban Planning, and Dreamscape Content Design are taught here.

t_ch: 后来苜蓿草家系投资入股，学校便加设了财富学院，教授经济学、会计学、金融信托学等知识。
t_en: Fortune Academy was added when the Alfalfa Family invested in the school as a stakeholder. Subjects such as Economics, Accountancy, and Finance and Trust Management are taught here.

t_ch: 可惜财富学院的课程不怎么注重德育，里面出过许多臭名昭著的星系级经济罪犯，被校友戏称为「最匹诺康尼」的学院——都进了监狱嘛。
t_en: Unfortunately, there isn't much emphasis placed on ethics in Fortune Academy's courses. As a result, many notorious galaxy-class financial criminals are alumni of the academy. This has led to it being jokingly referred to as the "most Penaconian" academy, as some of the students have actually become prisoners.

t_ch: 再往后，匹诺康尼成了盛会之星，谐乐学院也随之成立。它主要负责培育优秀的乐手、歌者和艺人，知更鸟小姐就是我们的名誉校友之一。
t_en: Later, Charmony Academy was established when Penacony became the Planet of Festivities. Charmony Academy is mainly responsible for nurturing talented musicians, singers, and artists. Miss Robin is one of our honorary alumni.

t_ch: 这三个学院的课你都可以去看看，说不定还能学到最新的「忆泡工业管线」知识，这在别的地方可学不到。
t_en: You can go check out the classes for all three academies. Perhaps you might even learn something about our newest subject, Industrial Pipeline of Memory Bubbles. This is knowledge that you'll not be able to obtain anywhere else.

t_ch: 先聊这些。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好，那你多保重…诶哟，我这老腰。
t_en: Okay, take care. *sigh* My back...

t_ch: 我还想和你聊聊。
t_en: I want to continue chatting with you.

t_ch: 说吧，正好我也歇歇腰。
t_en: Go ahead. It'll also be an opportunity for me to rest my back.

t_ch: 当校长是什么感觉？
t_en: What is it like being the dean?

t_ch: 校长啊…听起来是不是像个大官？其实也没想象中那么自在。至少在折纸大学，校长能决定的东西不是很多。
t_en: The dean, huh... Although it sounds like a powerful position, it is not as unrestrained as you would imagine. At least in Paperfold University, there isn't much that the dean actually has authority over.

t_ch: 对上，各大家系都密切关注着折纸大学的发展，那些权贵可不是校长能得罪起的……
t_en: Above the dean, the various Lineages are closely monitoring the developments in Paperfold University. The rich and powerful are not people that the dean can offend...

t_ch: 对下，专业的师资总共就那么些，校长敢摆架子，教授就敢跳槽……
t_en: Beneath the dean, there are only so many professional teachers. The professors will leave if they are unhappy with anything...

t_ch: 更别说每天批不完的请示和拿不到的经费了，谁想谁头疼。
t_en: This is even before mentioning the endless mountain of requests waiting for my approval and the funding that always seems to be far from reach. It would give any person a major headache.

t_ch: 不过没办法，必须要有一位校长。要是谁有能力替我头疼，这把交椅让给他也无妨。
t_en: But there's no way around it. The position of dean is a necessary one. If anyone is capable of taking over this headache from me, I wouldn't mind handing my position over to them.

t_en: What does Paperfold University teach?

t_ch: 折纸大学总共有三大学院：由鸢尾花家系赞助的谐乐学院，由隐夜鸫家系赞助的筑梦学院，以及由苜蓿草家系赞助的财富学院。

t_en: Unfortunately, there isn't much emphasis placed on ethics in Fortune Academy's courses. As a result, many notorious galaxy-class financial criminals are alumni of the academy. This has led to it being jokingly referred to as the "most Penaconian" academy, as some of the students have actually become prisoners.

t_ch: 先聊这些。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好，那你多保重…诶哟，我这老腰。
t_en: Okay, take care. *sigh* My back...

t_ch: 保重。
t_en: Take care.

t_ch: 你也是…诶哟，我这老腰。
t_en: You too. *sigh* My back...

t_ch: 平平无奇的垃圾桶，上面结了很厚的霜。
t_en: A trashcan covered in thick frost, not remarkable in any way.

t_ch: 你本想打开它一看究竟，但盖子冻得很严实。
t_en: You intend to open it, but its lid is frozen shut.

t_ch: 你又试了试，还是没法将它打开。不难看出，你依旧对垃圾桶爱得深沉。
t_en: You kept trying, but it wouldn't budge. Perhaps you are a bit too obsessed with this trashcan.

t_ch: 或许翻垃圾已经成了你的习惯，但有时人得学会收手。
t_en: Maybe rummaging through the trash has become a habit for you, but sometimes you've got to know when to quit.

t_ch: 就算再怎么坚持，你又能拿一个冻住的垃圾桶怎么办呢？
t_en: Even if you keep trying, what can you really do with a frozen trashcan?

t_ch: 整个搬走。
t_en: Take the entire trashcan away.

t_ch: 堂堂银河球棒侠当然不会被这点问题难倒——你将那垃圾桶从厚厚的积雪中拔出，力气之大甚至震开了它顽固不化的盖子。
t_en: Of course, the almighty Galactic Baseballer would not be deterred by a mere trashcan. You yank it out of the snow with all your might, its lid popping open.

t_ch: 垃圾倾泻而出，泼洒在你身后的地面上。倘若垃圾有眼睛，它们看你的眼神想必是幽怨且颤栗的。
t_en: The trash spills out onto the ground behind you, and if it were sentient, it would likely glare at you with a grudge and a shudder.

t_ch: 你战胜了顽强的垃圾桶。作为回报，你得到了一袋…不，是好几袋垃圾。
t_en: You have triumphed over the obstinate trashcan. As a reward for your resistance, you have acquired a bag... no, several bags of trash.

t_ch: 就此作罢。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 没错，走吧，就此离开吧。倘若你取走了垃圾，垃圾桶又该如何实现自我价值呢？
t_en: Yeah, right, just leave the trashcan be. If you removed all the trash, how would the trashcan fulfill its self-worth?

t_ch: 空空如也的垃圾桶，不知是哪位好心人清理的。
t_en: An empty trashcan, cleansed by some benevolent soul.

t_ch: 即使在最寒冷的冬夜，人们依旧可以围着它跳舞唱歌。
t_en: Even on the coldest winter nights, people could sing and dance around it.

t_ch: 工作台的主人暂时离开了，他最好是。上面还摆着组装了一半的零件呢。
t_en: The owner of this workbench is temporarily absent. They better return soon, for half-assembled parts are lying strewn upon it.

t_ch: 这台电梯被拦着是有原因的——果不其然，它坏了。
t_en: This elevator is blocked for a reason. Just as you've suspected, it is out of order.

t_ch: 这么多书放在这里肯定是有原因的，比如为了解闷——或者更糟，为了取暖。
t_en: There must be a reason why so many books are placed here. They could be used to whittle away the time... or even worse, to keep this place warm.

t_ch: 硕大的垃圾桶，被风雪爱抚后变得异常洁净。
t_en: A huge trashcan, buffeted by the caresses of wind and snow, appears surprisingly clean.

t_ch: 它的盖子被冻住了，或者干脆是从内向外开的。
t_en: Its lid is either frozen shut or opened from within.

t_ch: 这只垃圾桶实在过于庞大，你搬不动。
t_en: This trashcan is far too huge for you to move it.

t_ch: 你不禁陷入深思——究竟什么样的垃圾，才配得上这一路的冰封霜冻？
t_en: You can't help but wonder: What kind of trash deserves to be brought here, all the way through the blizzard?

t_ch: 北方的垃圾桶，比它南方的亲戚更加守口如瓶。
t_en: A trashcan dwelling in the north, seems more "tight-lipped" than its peers in the south.

t_ch: 工地上最常见的容器，有时被工人们用来藏匿贵重物品。
t_en: The most common container on construction sites. These barrels are occasionally used by workers to conceal their valuables.

t_ch: 你不确定眼前的这批木桶是否装有秘密。就算有，难道你打算将它取走吗？
t_en: You are uncertain if these barrels contain any treasures, and even if they do, are you truly intent on snatching them?

t_ch: 打开木桶。
t_en: Open a barrel.

t_ch: 你打开了第一个木桶。瞧，里面什么都没有。在这样忙碌且严肃的场合，谁会有闲心往木桶里藏东西呢？
t_en: You open the first barrel, and unsurprisingly, it is empty. Yeah, who would bother stashing something in a barrel during such a busy time?

t_ch: 再打开一个木桶。
t_en: Open another barrel.

t_ch: 你打开了第二个木桶，里面依旧什么也没有。看来这样的行为注定不会带给你任何东西。
t_en: You open the second barrel, and again, there's nothing inside. Your efforts seem destined to be fruitless.

t_ch: 说到底，一个人究竟要怀着怎样的信念与爱才会执着于每一件能互动的物品？
t_en: After all, how much obsession must one possess to interact with every random object they come across?

t_ch: 打开下一个木桶。
t_en: Open the next barrel.

t_ch: 你打开了第三个木桶，终于发现了某人藏匿于此的宝物——一瓶罐装快乐水。
t_en: You open the third barrel, and your perseverance is finally rewarded. You have discovered someone's treasure: A bottle of soda.

t_ch: 它的主人为什么把它放在这里？真相早已无从查证。现在你就是它的主人。
t_en: Why would someone hide a bottle of soda here? The truth no longer matters now. What matters is that it is now yours.

t_ch: 满足吧，庆贺吧，勇者的行径终于得到了回报。
t_en: Rejoice in jubilation! The hero's efforts have finally reaped rewards!

t_ch: 但...你的「开拓」精神并没有平息。难不成还有其他秘密等待被发现？
t_en: However... your ardor as a Trailblazer has yet to be quenched. Who knows what other secrets await you?

t_ch: 打开最后一个木桶！
t_en: Open the last barrel!

t_ch: 你挪开提灯，把手伸向最后一个木桶。一股熟悉而陌生的味道扑面而来。
t_en: You move the lamp aside and reach for the last barrel. As you open its lid, a familiar yet strange smell assails you.

t_ch: 垃圾，准确的说——被冰镇过的垃圾。
t_en: It smells like... trash. More precisely, a mound of frozen trash.

t_ch: 居然有人把它当成了垃圾桶？真差劲。但也许这正是它吸引你的原因。
t_en: Someone used this barrel as a trashcan? How outrageous! But perhaps, this is precisely why it appeals to you.

t_ch: 满意离去。
t_en: Leave in satisfaction.

t_ch: 如果要问有什么比罐装快乐水更值得快乐，那一定是冰镇过的罐装快乐水。
t_en: If there's any treasure that's even better than bottled soda, it would be iced bottled soda.

t_ch: 你满意地带着战利品离去，这片土地上又多了一个快乐的人。
t_en: Satisfied, you depart with your newfound loot. This world has witnessed a new merry soul.

t_ch: 就此离去。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 明智的选择。收手吧，阿棒。这个世界还有其他美好等待你去体验。
t_en: A wise decision. Just let it go, batter. This world has countless other wonders for you to explore.

t_ch: 就此离去。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 明智的选择。收手吧，阿棒。这个世界还有其他美好等待你去体验。
t_en: A wise decision. Just let it go, batter. This world has countless other wonders for you to explore.

t_ch: 转身离去。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 明智的选择。收手吧，阿棒。这个世界还有其他美好等待你去体验。
t_en: A wise decision. Just let it go, batter. This world has countless other wonders for you to explore.

t_ch: 工地上最常见的容器，有时被当作箱子，有时被当作凳子。
t_en: The most common container on construction sites, these barrels are sometimes repurposed as makeshift chests or stools.

t_ch: 你永远也猜不到人们会把什么东西放进这些木桶里。
t_en: One could never know what people might stash away in these barrels.

t_ch: 这种木桶是如此常见，以至于工地上几乎每人都可以分到一个。
t_en: These barrels are so commonly seen on the construction site that almost every worker has one.

t_ch: 那盏灯之所以一直在这，是因为每个借灯的人都能记得把它放回原位。
t_en: This lamp remains in its current position because anyone who borrows it knows to return it to its original spot.

t_ch: 事物不是一成不变的——就像这些木桶不会一直空着一样。
t_en: Things are forever changing, much like how these barrels will not remain empty indefinitely.

t_ch: 场馆还在布置中，请您之后再来。
t_en: The venue is still being set up. Please come back later.

t_ch: 「星际决胜庆典」绝赞举办中。如果你也想成为以太战线大师的话，就赶快来报名参赛吧。
t_en: The Interastral Tournament Festival is on. Come and sign up for the contest if you want to become a master Aetherium Wars player.

t_ch: 这或许是场馆内位置最佳的观景台，所有景色尽收眼底，而且完全免费。
t_en: This observation deck is probably the best spot in the venue. Here you can get awesome views, and the best part is: It's totally free.

t_ch: 从这里向下望去，人就像蚂蚁一样渺小。
t_en: When you look down from here, people are as tiny as ants.

t_ch: 这个故事告诉我们，最好的体验不一定非要花钱才能得到。
t_en: This tale shows that the best experiences don't always have to come with a price tag.

t_ch: 从山巅俯瞰山脚的战斗，真能提起兴趣吗？
t_en: Can you really enjoy a battle unfolding at the foot of a mountain while standing at the summit?

t_ch: 在这个位置，就连观众的喝彩声都变得模糊了。
t_en: When you stand at this spot, even the cheers from the spectators become indistinct.

t_ch: 当你沐浴在喝彩中时，想必也有人这样远远观望着你。
t_en: As you bask in the applause, you imagine that there must be someone observing you from afar as well.

t_ch: 铁丝网后一片黑暗，不知隐藏着怎样的秘密。
t_en: Beyond the wires of the fence lies darkness, and who knows what secrets may be concealed there.

t_ch: 传说竞技场内潜伏着一头可怕的怪兽。倘若它真的存在，估计也就是玩家眼中又一只有待收服的以太灵罢了。
t_en: Legend has it that there's a fearsome beast lurking within the arena. However, if it truly exists, it's likely just another Aether Spirit to be conquered and tamed by the players.

t_ch: 这是机关能源中枢，可以将地髓矿化作电力，负责整个场馆的能源供应。
t_en: This mechanism energy hub transforms Geomarrow into electricity, thus providing power for the entire venue.

t_ch: 在玩家们尽情对战、肆意狂欢的同时，是工人的劳动在背后默默支持。
t_en: It is the tireless efforts of the staff that enable players to enjoy thrilling battles and wanton merriment.

t_ch: 在某场比赛开始前，一位作弊者获取了对手的终端，将其以太灵更改成了最弱的配置。
t_en: Earlier, before a certain match began, a deceitful player stole their opponent's terminal and replaced their Aether Spirits with the weakest ones possible.

t_ch: 自那之后，主办方就将上场通道彼此分隔，以免选手提前接触。
t_en: Ever since that incident, participants from both sides have been kept separate to prevent any unwanted contact between them.

t_ch: 作为旧日的武器试验场，有些废弃零件堆在这里也是很正常的。
t_en: It's not surprising to see discarded parts piled up in this abandoned weapon testing ground.

t_ch: 这个箱子被放在了不起眼的角落里，仿佛故意不让人发现似的。难道是为了掩饰什么？
t_en: The box has been tucked away in an obscure corner, as if someone intentionally meant to keep it out of sight. Is there anything unspeakable hidden inside?

t_ch: 你掀开盖子一看，里面凌乱地塞满了照片，记录着各选手比赛时的英姿。
t_en: As you open the box, you're greeted with a spread of photos showcasing the valor of various players during their matches.

t_ch: 大部分照片被不礼貌地打上了叉，看来拍摄者对它们并不满意，不知是因为选手还是摄影技术。
t_en: Most of the photos are crossed out, as if the photographer wasn't satisfied with them. You're not sure if it's due to the players or the quality of the photos themselves.

t_ch: 这个箱子被放在了不起眼的角落里。它的盖子并未合拢，似乎有人动过手脚。
t_en: The box has been tucked away in an obscure corner, and its lid has been left open as if it was tampered with.

t_ch: 你掀开盖子一看，里面凌乱地塞满了照片——你的照片。
t_en: As you open the box, you're greeted with a mess of photos — and they're all of you.

t_ch: 这些照片从各种角度拍摄了你对战时的英姿，其中甚至有许多面部特写。
t_en: These photos showcase you from various angles, and there are even a lot of close-ups of your face.

t_ch: 不难想象，照片的拍摄者按下快门时一定怀着十分...热烈的情感。
t_en: Obviously, the photographer must have taken these photos with... intense passion.

t_ch: 这个忆泡突兀地出现在这里，七彩的光华流溢其中。不知为何，你感觉它似乎和你在空间站见过的不尽相同。
t_en: This Memory Bubble suddenly appeared here, overflowing with iridescent light. You can't say why, but this one seems a little different to the others you've seen in the space station.

t_ch: 你靠近忆泡，感到一丝凉意。两个熟悉的身影涌入你的脑海......
t_en: As you approach the Memory Bubble, you feel a slight chill in the air, as two familiar figures surge into your mind...

t_ch: 真没想到，最后的神秘玩家居然是虎克...哦，应该叫「漆黑的虎克大人」才对！难道这也在你的安排之中吗，乔瓦尼老哥？
t_en: Who could have guessed, the final mystery player was Hook... Oh, I mean Pitch-Dark Hook the Great! Was this all part of your plan, too, Giovanni?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，怎么可能？相比人为编排的剧目，我更喜欢看到事件自然发展出的「欢愉」结局。
t_en: Hahaha, how could it be? I much prefer the "Elation" of an ending that has come about naturally over a choreographed drama.

t_ch: 不过，桑博兄弟，同为「假面愚者」，你似乎从来不愿分享自己的{RUBY_B#欢愉}美学{RUBY_E#}见解…这又是为什么？
t_en: Although, brother Sampo, as a fellow Masked Fool, I feel like you have never shared your own views of {RUBY_B#Elation}aesthetics{RUBY_E#}... Why is that?

t_ch: 呃，让我想想怎么说比较不得罪人...这么说吧，在咱们这群人里，有那么几位不是很懂得「把握分寸」…我就不直呼其名了哈。
t_en: Hmm, let me think how to explain without offending anyone... I'll put it this way: In our group, there are some who can't "read the room"... I won't mention them by name, of course.

t_ch: …我可不喜欢跟那些家伙打交道，多没劲！每个人对于「欢愉」的追求都不一样，何必费那力气把自己的审美强加给别人呢？嘿……
t_en: ...I just don't like communicating with people like that, it's so dull! Everyone has their own way of pursuing Elation. Why must I waste my own energy sharing my sense of aesthetics with others? Heh...

t_ch: 太过重口味的玩笑会带来麻烦；这麻烦事一多，有趣的事情就...不再有趣了。你懂我意思吧？
t_en: A joke with too heavy a tone can bring trouble. As this trouble builds, what was once funny becomes... no longer fun. You understand what I mean, don't you?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我理解了...话说回来，「那位」似乎正准备前往匹诺康尼。你难道不打算...？
t_en: Hahaha, I understand. Now that you say it, "that" person seems to be heading to Penacony. Are you not planning to...?

t_ch: 呃…要是可以的话，我宁愿这辈子都不再跟她打交道......
t_en: Hah... If I could, I would never speak to her again...

t_ch: 在逐渐模糊的声音中，忆泡破裂了，那光在你眼前转瞬即逝。
t_en: As the voices become faded, the Memory Bubble bursts and the light dissipates before your eyes.

t_ch: 也许这枚忆泡在外面太久、已经失去了活性；也许是留下这段信息的人认为你已经看完了关键部分......
t_en: Perhaps this Memory Bubble was left out for too long and has lost its vitality... Or perhaps, whoever left it here feels that you have already seen what is important...

t_ch: 谜团似乎越来越多，但有句箴言永远适用：跟戴面具的人打交道，一定要留个心眼。
t_en: The mysteries seem to just be piling up, but one phrase rings true: "When talking to one who wears a mask, always keep your wits about you."

t_ch: 这座桥梁的结构不太稳定，在修缮结束前请不要靠近。
t_en: This bridge is structurally unstable, please stay clear until repairs are complete.

t_ch: 奉大守护者之命，前方区域不予开放。
t_en: By the order of the Supreme Guardian, the area ahead is closed.
Supplies... delivery... work... assistance...

t_ch: 还是先不要打扰它了。
t_en: Maybe I should leave it alone.

t_ch: 啊，是你啊，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Ah, it's you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 最近情况怎么样？
t_en: How have things been lately?

t_ch: 好多了。虽然算不上事事顺意，但跟过去比起来，一切都在慢慢变好。
t_en: Much better. Though not everything is going smoothly, things are slowly taking a turn for the better.

t_ch: 唯一的麻烦是公司那帮家伙。自从上次的事结束后，这里的黑衣人越来越多，其中有些干脆住下来不走了。
t_en: The only trouble is those IPC guys. Ever since the Eternal Freeze let up, there have been more and more of those people in black around here. Some of them have simply settled in and not left!

t_ch: 外星人是指我吗？
t_en: By alien, do you mean me?

t_ch: 嘿哟，你可是大守护者的朋友、贝洛伯格的救星，怎么能算外人呢？
t_en: Oh no! You're a friend of the Supreme Guardian and savior of Belobog, how could I treat you as an outsider?

t_ch: 是公司那帮家伙。自从上次的事结束后，这里的黑衣人越来越多，其中有些干脆住下来不走了。
t_en: It's those IPC guys. Ever since the Eternal Freeze let up, there have been more and more of those people in black around here. Some of them have simply settled in and not left!

t_ch: 虽然大守护者说不必紧张，可毕竟那群人打的是整个星球的主意。我还是不太信任他们。
t_en: The Supreme Guardian said it was no reason to panic, but they were planning to take our whole planet! I still don't quite trust them...

t_ch: 大不了就再打一架。
t_en: If it bothers you, then fight them again!

t_ch: 哈哈，说得对。贝洛伯格的人民从不惧怕纷争。
t_en: Haha, quite right! The people of Belobog do not fear battle!

t_ch: 上次我们已经把他们的底牌逼了出来，这次看谁能笑到最后！
t_en: Last time we got them on the ropes, let's see who gets the last laugh this time!

t_ch: 贝洛伯格需要他们的技术。
t_en: Belobog needs their technology.

t_ch: 你说得对，但这不会是他们胡作非为的理由。
t_en: You're right, but that's no excuse for them to do what they want.

t_ch: 我会紧盯着他们的动向。要是还一肚子坏水，就给我卷铺盖回家！
t_en: I'll keep a close eye on their movements. If I find out they're still up to no good, I'll send them packing!

t_ch: 我会好好监督这帮黑衣人的。
t_en: I'll keep a close eye on these people in black.

t_ch: 你看远处的山，居然都没有那台机器人大。
t_en: Look at those mountains in the distance, they're not even as tall as that robot!

t_ch: 这么大的机器人，也不知要修到什么时候去。我还有可能看见那天吗.......
t_en: I have no idea how long it will take to fix a robot that large. I wonder if I'll even get to see the day...

t_ch: ...我可不是要偷懒的意思。
t_en: ...I'm not trying to slack off.

t_ch: 不许告我的状啊，小咪。
t_en: Please don't tell on me, Mimi.

t_ch: 没想到这趟班站了这么久，早知道就该吃了饭再来。
t_en: I didn't realize I'd be standing so long on this shift. I'd have eaten before I came if I knew!

t_ch: 就快换班了，到时候可以一起去吃。
t_en: It's almost time for the shift to change. We can go grab something together then.

t_ch: 好啊，我要吃酸汤夕红鱼。
t_en: Alright, I want redsunset salmon in sour soup!

t_ch: 你居然不吃甜汤？异端！
t_en: Sour instead of sweet soup? Heresy!

t_ch: 酸汤才是全银河系统一认证的做法！不信你去问那些公司的员工！
t_en: Sour soup is the only recipe recognized across the entire cosmos! If you don't believe me, go and ask the IPC employees!

s_ch: 站岗的卫兵&站岗的铁卫
s_en: Guard on Sentry Duty & Silvermane Guard on Sentry Duty
t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: Hahahaha!

t_ch: 在一片欢乐的气氛里，那名来自下城区的卫兵却转过身子，哽咽起来。
t_en: Among the joyous atmosphere, the guard from the Underworld suddenly turns around and chokes up.

t_ch: 喂，你怎么了？
t_en: Hey, what's wrong with you?

t_ch: 没事，我只是在想......
t_en: Nothing, I was just thinking...

t_ch: 如果不是封锁令解除了，咱们俩可没机会像这样说话。
t_en: If the lockdown had never been lifted, we'd never have had a chance to talk like this.

t_ch: 都过去了。
t_en: That's all in the past, now.

t_ch: 哪里有星光？怎么什么也没有啾！
t_en: Where's the starlight? Why can't I see anything, tweet?

t_ch: 黑漆漆的，说好的「空艇十大打卡地」呢？
t_en: It's completely dark in here! Isn't this one of the airship's ten must-see attractions?

t_ch: 你把头埋在这儿干嘛？
t_en: Why are you burying your head in here?

t_ch: 啾啾，人家的头好痛，怎么回事？
t_en: Tweet tweet. My head's throbbing. What's going on?

t_ch: …怎么是沙滩椅在说话。
t_en: ...Is that deckchair talking?

t_ch: 啊，原来是这样啾？！搞了半天是我把头卡在椅子里了。
t_en: Tweet? So that's what happened! My head was stuck inside a chair!

t_ch: 难怪没看到星光…要不是你，人家的大头差点就变成了躺椅的永久装饰品啾！
t_en: No wonder I couldn't see any starlight! If it weren't for you, my big head would have become a permanent decoration on this deckchair!

t_ch: 再见啦，人家要去打卡啦！不过…人家会记住你的！有机会的话，一起来晒星光啾！
t_en: I'm off for some sightseeing now, but don't worry, I won't forget you! Let's go enjoy the starlight together sometime, tweet! See you around!

t_ch: 被你发现了啾！恭喜你，有缘人！欢迎光临「{TEXTJOIN#87}」最神奇的路边摊！
t_en: You found me, tweet! Congratulations, lucky one! Welcome to the most magical stall on {TEXTJOIN#87}!

t_ch: 我卖的都是我的珍～藏～，只有有缘的人才有购买资格啾！
t_en: I only sell pieces from my most precious collections here! Only those lucky enough to find this place are granted the rare chance to buy them!

t_ch: 有多神奇，让我看看！
t_en: How magical is "magical"? Let me see!

t_ch: 你不打算买的话，就别看了啾！我这里可是游客梦寐以求的宝贝！不能轻易示人的！
t_en: If you're not looking to buy, then don't bother looking, tweet! I only sell the most coveted treasures here. Not just anybody gets to see them!

t_ch: 豪华空艇上竟然还有路边摊…
t_en: I can't believe there are hawkers on such a luxurious airship...

t_ch: 神奇羽毛，限量出售啾～只卖给独具慧眼的有缘人～不识货的不要来～
t_en: Magical feathers for sale, tweet! Limited quantities available only! Exclusively for those with an eye for the finer things in life! Don't bother asking if that's not you!

t_ch: 我卖的是羽～毛～只有最识货——诶，我的羽毛呢？
t_en: Feathers for sale! Only for true connoisseurs of... Huh? Where are my feathers?

t_ch: 哎呀，这下丢人丢大发了，我忘记把我的尾羽带过来了啾～
t_en: Oh, how embarrassing! I forgot the tail feathers, tweet!

t_ch: 让客人白跑一趟真的很抱歉，我得回去找一下了啾！
t_en: I'm so sorry for the wasted trip, dear guest. I need to go back and find them, tweet!

t_ch: 啾！朋友，请离边缘远一点，风大会很危险的啾！
t_en: Tweet! Stay away from the edge, buddy! It can be pretty dangerous when it gets windy, tweet!

t_ch: 空艇上虽然风景美丽，但安全总是第一位的啾！
t_en: While the view from the airship is stunning, safety always takes priority, tweet!

t_ch: 我只是想看看风景。
t_en: I just wanted to see the view.

t_ch: 风景固然美好，但生命更宝贵啾。请退后一些，这样我也能安心啾！
t_en: The view may be stunning, but your life matters more. Please step back, so I don't have to worry, tweet!

t_ch: 我只是想看看你。
t_en: I just wanted to see you.

t_ch: 你怎么这么会说话，我会不好意思的啾！
t_en: Aww, you have such a way with words. I'm gonna blush, tweet!

t_ch: 我在这里就是要确保大家的人身安全，以免发生意外！
t_en: My job is to ensure everyone's safety and prevent any accidents!

t_ch: 谢谢理解啾！我要去别的地方检查了啾！
t_en: Thanks for understanding, tweet! I'm off to patrol other areas now, tweet!

t_ch: 你的余光瞥见<color=#dbc291ff>天花板上好像有什么东西</color>垂了下来。
t_en: Out of the corner of your eye, you notice <color=#dbc291ff>something dangling from the ceiling</color>.

t_ch: 啾啾！啾啾…
t_en: Tweet tweet! Tweet tweet...

t_ch: （好像有小鸟在叫，是在喊我吗？但这附近也没看到有鸟的痕迹…难道在外面？还是用<color=#dbc291ff>望远镜</color>观察一下吧。）
t_en: (It sounds like there's a bird chirping. Is it calling out to me? But I don't see any signs of a bird nearby... Could it be outside? Better use the <color=#dbc291ff>telescope</color> to check.)

t_ch: 啾！你好，这是小鸟特别的锻炼方式——天际倒立！
t_en: Tweet! Hello there! This is a special exercise for birds. It's called the Horizon Topsy-Turvy!

t_ch: 倒立可以让血液流向大脑，增强思考能力，还能锻炼平衡感呢。
t_en: Going upside down can improve blood flow to the brain, boost mental capacities, and enhance sense of balance.

t_ch: 这样不会头晕吗？
t_en: Don't you get dizzy doing that?

t_ch: …这个…刚开始会有一点，但久了就习惯了。
t_en: ...Well, a bit at first. But I've gotten used to it over time.

t_ch: 天花板有什么好看的？
t_en: But isn't it boring just staring at the ceiling?

t_ch: 没找到合适的角度自然看不到啾！学会倒立，世界会别有一番风味啾！
t_en: You just need the right angle, tweet! Learn to go upside down, and you'll see the world from a whole new perspective!

t_ch: 从这里看下去，每个人都是一颗颗忙碌的小星星！奇怪，我的视线里怎么会有这么多星星在转圈……
t_en: From this angle, everyone looks like a busy little star! Weird, why are there so many stars circling in my vision...

t_ch: 练腿时间结束，现在我要去练嘴了！
t_en: Now that my leg workout is done, it's time for beak exercises!

t_ch: 希望下次见到你时，你也能加入健身的行列，跟我一起倒立！
t_en: I hope the next time I see you, you'll join our fitness crew and try doing headstands with me!

t_ch: 嘿，朋友，你没看到我在这儿吗？为什么要把我的坐骑打破？你这样会影响到我的督导工作的！
t_en: Hey, friend! Didn't you see me? Why did you break my mount? That's gonna get in the way of my overseeing duties!

t_ch: 你在这干吗？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我在这儿守着，确保没有人插队的啾！
t_en: I'm keeping watch to make sure no one jumps the line, tweet!

t_ch: 什么坐骑？
t_en: What mount?

t_ch: 我的坐骑啾，可以让我漂浮在这里…虽说这点小高度对我来说算不了什么！但是要保持长期滞空的话我的腿会受不了的。
t_en: My mount, tweet! It's what keeps me airborne here. Don't get me wrong, I can handle the height, but hovering for too long tires out my legs.

t_ch: 好啦，我得再找个新地方监督大家了，你在空艇上玩的话记得要排队啾！这样每个人才能有机会享受空艇的游乐设施啾！
t_en: Alright, I need to find a new spot to oversee things. Just make sure to line up when you're having fun in the airship so that everyone gets a chance to experience the recreational facilities, tweet!

t_ch: 啾啾，欢迎来到匹诺康尼人气最高的餐厅！请问您有订位吗，没有订位的话不可以来这儿啾？
t_en: Tweet tweet! Welcome to Penacony's most popular restaurant! Do you have a reservation? If not, I'm afraid you can't dine here, tweet.

t_ch: …哈哈，开玩笑的。我不是服务员啾，我只是来随便玩玩的！
t_en: Haha, just kidding! I'm not a waitress, tweet. I'm just goofing around!

t_ch: 你这样开玩笑不太礼貌吧。
t_en: That's not very nice.

t_ch: 啾，你说得对。我只是太喜欢这里的味道了，香喷喷的菜肴味，要是能吃上两口就更好了啾！
t_en: You're right, tweet. It's just that the food smells so good here. I wish I could have a bite or two, tweet!

t_ch: 你这样别人还吃不吃饭了？
t_en: Aren't you in the way of the diners?

t_ch: 啾啾，我保证不会打扰任何人的。我只是想把餐巾摆成我的形状，不会触碰任何食物的啾。
t_en: Tweet tweet! I won't disturb anyone, I promise! I just wanted to fold the napkins into a shape that looks like me. I won't touch any of the food, tweet.

t_ch: 这里不需要订位的啾，你是要吃饭吗？那我还是不打扰你了，再见啾！
t_en: Anyway, you don't need a reservation to dine here. I'll stop bothering you if you're here for a meal. Bye, tweet!

t_ch: 等上菜的期间，可以好好欣赏我的杰作——这几张餐巾可都是我亲自折起来的，是不是很好看啾！
t_en: Take a good look at my masterpieces while you're waiting to be served. I folded these napkins myself! Aren't they nice, tweet?

t_ch: 干嘛把我拽出来呀，人家正听到故事发展的关键时刻呢！
t_en: Why did you pull me out? I just got to the good part of the story!

t_ch: 你这样会打扰到别人的。
t_en: You're being a bother.

t_ch: 人类应该不会介意我偷听的吧？人类好像没有这种规则啾……
t_en: Humans won't mind if I listen in, right? I don't think they have a rule against it, tweet.

t_ch: 我前几天就看到，有个人答应另一个人，拍着胸脯保证自己「绝对不会告诉别人」，结果转头就把同伴的秘密告诉给别人啾。
t_en: A few days ago, I saw someone swear that they would never tell their friend's secret to anyone, then immediately spilled the beans to someone else. Tweet!

t_ch: 偷听别人讲话不好吧。
t_en: Eavesdropping isn't right.

t_ch: 啾啾，我只是悄悄地听，保证不影响他们的。啾，故事有时候就像风，随处都可捕捉啾，小鸟闻风而动是我们的天性啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet. I'm just quietly listening. They won't even know I'm here. A story is like the wind sometimes. It can rise in any corner of the world, and I'll follow it wherever it blows! That's the nature of us birds, tweet!

t_ch: 糟了，我怎么感觉有人在盯着我看啾？我可受不了这种目光洗礼…我先走了啾！
t_en: Oh no, I can feel someone staring at me, tweet! I hate this kind of attention. I'm outta here, tweet!

t_ch: 再见啾！我得赶回去把听到的八卦分享给我的同伴了！
t_en: Bye, tweet! Gotta bring the latest gossip to all my friends now!

t_ch: 嗨～惊喜不惊喜，意外不意外～
t_en: Surprise! Bet you didn't expect to find me!

t_ch: 遇到我这样的贵宾～是不是很开心～
t_en: Aren't you glad you found me?

t_ch: 一点～也～不～惊～喜～
t_en: Not. One. Bit.

t_ch: 真的吗？我不信！像我这么可爱的小鸟，怎么会不受欢迎！
t_en: Really? Lies! Why wouldn't you when I'm such a cute little birdie?

t_ch: 大家打开箱子只想看到钱…
t_en: I expect to see cold hard cash when I open chests...

t_ch: 纸钞哪里比得上本鸟，我会突然冒出来，还会啾啾叫。哼哼！
t_en: Banknotes? Hmph, I'm a far better find than paper money. I pop out for surprises, and I even tweet!

t_ch: 真是搞不懂你们人类，为什么总是盯着纸钞不放？这种长方形的纸片就这么迷人吗？
t_en: I don't get you humans. Why are you all always obsessing over banknotes? Are rectangular pieces of paper that attractive?

t_ch: 我怎么不觉得…反倒是脏兮兮的，害我羽毛都弄脏了。
t_en: Can't say I see the appeal... They're kind of filthy. They even got my feathers all gross.

t_ch: 我得回去梳洗一下了，希望下次能带给你真正的惊喜啾！
t_en: Gotta go preen my feathers now. I'll give you a proper surprise next time, tweet!

t_ch: 本船长有令：舵轮往左打，满舵全速前进，冲冲冲！
t_en: Captain's orders! Hard to port! Full speed ahead! Go, go, go!

t_ch: 全体船员都要听令啾！
t_en: All hands on deck, tweet!

t_ch: 你搞清楚谁才是船长没有？
t_en: Do you even know who's captain here?

t_ch: 啾啾，我心中都有个船长梦，我也可以当船长啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet. I've always dreamed of being captain. I'm totally captain material, tweet!

t_ch: 你还懂航行？
t_en: You know how to sail?

t_ch: 啾啾～舵轮转呀转～我就是新任船长～见过无数风浪～我要为大家领航～
t_en: Tweet tweet! Turn the wheel! I'm the new captain now~ Many a storm I've weathered, and safely I'll sail you all through~

t_ch: 你是船长，那我是什么？
t_en: If you're the captain, what does that make me?

t_ch: 啾！对不起，是我没注意，你才是船长啾。那我把船长这个职位还给你，我去当你的大副怎么样？啊，已经有大副了，好可惜啾……
t_en: Tweet? Oh, you're the captain! I'm sorry, I should've realized sooner, tweet. In that case, you can have the position back and I'll be your first mate? ...Aw, you already have one. That's a shame, tweet.

t_ch: 啾啾！我忘记去招募船员了，原来是我是光杆船长…好丢人，亏我还那么高兴……
t_en: Ah, I forgot to recruit crew members! Tweet tweet! A captain without a ship... How embarrassing. And here I was so excited...

t_ch: 啊呀呀呀我的脸怎么这么烫，不行了我要回去把头埋在羽毛下。
t_en: Oh no, I can feel my cheeks burning. I'm going to bury my head underneath my wings for a while.

t_ch: 刚才的事…还有我说的话你就当没发生过吧啾，不许跟别的小鸟讲喔！
t_en: Just pretend none of that ever happened... And don't tell the other birds!

t_ch: 要老实排队啾～
t_en: Gotta be good and stay in queue, tweet!

t_ch: 所以我们到底在排什么啾？
t_en: Tweet, what are we queuing for, actually?

t_ch: 啾？你们不是来打卡的吗？
t_en: Tweet? You're not here to check the place out?

t_ch: 我只是看你们在排队才过来的……
t_en: I only came because I saw you all in queue...

t_ch: 是在观景啾，别急，马上到你。
t_en: We're queuing to sightsee, tweet. Don't worry, it'll be your turn soon.

t_ch: …不会只有我恐高吧……
t_en: I can't be the only one afraid of heights, right...?

t_ch: 你们站那么高，爪子不会发软吗？
t_en: Don't your claws go weak standing so high up?

t_ch: 欢迎来到～啾～
t_en: Tweet! Welcome~

t_ch: 小鸟乐园～啾啾～
t_en: To bird paradise! Tweet tweet~

t_ch: …你跳，那我也跳……
t_en: ...If you jump, I jump.

t_ch: 为什么要跳，人家会飞啾。
t_en: Why are we jumping? We can fly, tweet.

t_ch: 你真是不懂浪漫啊……
t_en: You're so not a romantic...

t_ch: 浪漫是什么，人家不知道啾。
t_en: What's a romantic? I don't get it, tweet.

t_ch: 别站那么高，很危险的，快下来！
t_en: Don't go so high up, it's dangerous! Come down here!

t_ch: 我才不怕，站得高看得远才够爽！
t_en: I'm not scared. The view is better up here!

t_ch: 风那么大吹着脸不疼吗？
t_en: But it's so windy. Doesn't it make your face sting?

t_ch: 你懂什么，跟挠痒痒一样。
t_en: Don't be silly. It just tickles.

t_ch: 你隐约记得，似乎在空间站的某处见到过黑塔的手稿，但那时只有寥寥数页。
t_en: You vaguely remember seeing one of Herta's manuscripts somewhere around the space station, but back then it was only a few measly pages.

t_ch: 没想到转眼间，新书出版广告都挂了出来，你觉得——
t_en: Who'd have thought you'd see adverts promoting the new book in what seems like the blink of an eye. You think...

t_ch: 该去要个签名版了。
t_en: I could go get myself an autographed copy.

t_ch: 以你和黑塔的关系，你觉得你该去要个签名版了，这玩意绝对是抢手货，想来她不会拒绝你的——
t_en: You should be able to score an autographed copy of the book based on your connection with Herta. That'll be hot commodity, for sure. She wouldn't refuse you, would she...

t_ch: 但万一…黑塔真的拒绝了你，你该怎么办？大脑轻微宕机了一秒，但转念一想，只要你想要，总会有办法的，你对此深以为然。
t_en: But just in case... What would you do if Herta did indeed refuse you? Your brain shuts down for a split second, then suddenly thinks better of it — There will always be a way, so long as you want it. You're convinced of that.

t_ch: 好奇新书什么时候发售。
t_en: I wonder when her new book is coming out.

t_ch: 广告内容并未提及新书的发售时间，只用了一个含糊的「稳定更新」遮掩过去，而且摆放的地段也……
t_en: The adverts make no mention of any release date for the book, instead referring cryptically to a "stable update." And as for the location where it's placed...

t_ch: 「该不会没有下文了吧？」你试着揣测黑塔的作风，但很快又将这个念头从脑子里摘了出去，因为以黑塔的性格，她可能只会公开一章「作者序」就作罢了。
t_en: "Is that all she wrote?" You try guessing at Herta's style but quickly erase that idea from your mind — because Herta very well might just have published an author's preface and left it at that.

t_ch: 你好奇黑塔究竟是如何敷衍公司的。
t_en: You are curious about how on earth Herta manages to phone it in to the IPC.

t_ch: 你无所畏惧，时刻践行着「开拓」之道——你决定直接发消息问问黑塔本人。
t_en: Without a shred of fear, you always practice the way of the Trailblaze — You decide to send a message directly to Herta herself.

t_ch: 这会是个升降台吗？就像影视作品里常见的那样——在感应到主人靠近时，从某处升起一套合金装甲，又或者是巨大战舰的外置驾驶舱。
t_en: Is this an elevated platform? Just like in the movies and cartoons, a set of alloyed armor pops out of somewhere once the owner comes within proximity — or it might be the external cockpit of a massive warship.

t_ch: 亦或是个实验舱？藏匿着「繁育」的虫卵，莽撞的外来者在不经意间将危害世界的灾难释放出来……
t_en: Or is it a research pod? One holding Propagation eggs that any reckless outsiders could inadvertently unleash to imperil the entire universe...

t_ch: 你决定——
t_en: You decide to...

t_ch: 伸手触碰它。
t_en: Reach out and touch it.

t_ch: 你伸手触碰它的表面，起初，泛着微光的冰冷表面在你指尖留下冰冷的触感。
t_en: You reach out and touch its surface. At first, its cold surface leaves behind a chilly sensation on your fingers.

t_ch: 忽然间，它冰冷坚固的外壳消失了，你的手指失去了依靠。一股奇怪的吸力传来，你的手指连带着整个身躯，向前栽去——
t_en: But the cold shell suddenly disappears, leaving your hand without any support. Drawn by a strange force that sucks you past the threshold, your fingers and the rest of your body begin to fall forward...

t_ch: 你被关进了实验舱中，那股吸力仍未消失，不断抽取着舱中的空气。
t_en: You are trapped inside the research pod, and that sucking sound does not abate as it continues vacuuming the air out of the pod.

t_ch: 肺部的撕裂感愈演愈烈，或许在几分钟后你就会休克，永远地被封装在这里。
t_en: The tearing sensation in your lungs grows worse and worse. You may end up going into shock in a few minutes, sealing your fate here permanently.

t_ch: 你拼尽全力地大声求救——
t_en: You scream out for help with all of your might...

t_ch: 帮帮我，黑塔女士！
t_en: Help me, Madam Herta!

t_ch: 你努力喊出黑塔的名字，试图向空间站的主人求救。
t_en: You shout out Herta's name, trying to seek help from the owner of the space station.

t_ch: 帮帮我，阮•梅女士！
t_en: Help me, Miss Ruan Mei!

t_ch: 在最后几分钟倒计时里，意识开始迟缓，脑海中却不知为何浮现出了阮•梅的样貌。
t_en: During the final moments of the countdown, your consciousness begins to fade — but somehow, Ruan Mei's visage pops into your head.

t_ch: 这里似乎是她的实验室？你不合时宜的困惑剥夺了所剩无几的求生欲。
t_en: This seems to be her laboratory? Your poorly-timed recollection saps whatever will to live you had remaining.

t_ch: 最先消失的是视觉，而后你听见一阵脚步声传来。
t_en: Your vision goes first. You then hear the sound of footsteps approaching.

t_ch: 是谁？是布置陷阱的凶手？还是这座空间站的主人？
t_en: Who is that? The crook who laid out these traps? Or the owner of this space station?

t_ch: 是黑塔，还是艾丝妲，亦或是…阮•梅？这会是意识溃散前的幻觉吗？
t_en: Is it Herta, or is it Asta, or could it be... Ruan Mei? Are these just hallucinations before you pass out?

t_ch: 那股吸力消失了——
t_en: The suctioning force dissipates...
